title: Protective/Restraining Orders - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Legal & Public Safety > Protective/Restraining Orders 26 Matching Service Providers CENTER AGAINST RAPE AND DOMESTIC VIOLENCE DOMESTIC VIOLENCE & SEXUAL ASSAULT SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides emergency shelter, sexual assault response and hospital advocacy, and legal advocacy and assistance with restraining and stalking order paperwork. Volunteer opportunities available. 2208 SW 3rd Street Corvallis, OR 97333 Eligibility: Shelter: Age 18 and older, emancipated minors, and minors accompanied by an adult fleeing a violent situation, who are in immediate danger All other service: Varies by service Hours: Phone: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Live Chat: Monday-Friday 10am-1pm Email (541) 758-0219 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CLACKAMAS WOMEN'S SERVICES DOMESTIC VIOLENCE AND SEXUAL ASSAULT SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a crisis line, confidential text and chat line, emergency shelter and transitional housing services, legal advocacy to assist with temporary restraining orders, safety planning, housing advocacy and financial empowerment programs, violence prevention, and community education. 256 Warner Milne Road Oregon City, OR 97045 Eligibility: Survivors of domestic violence, sexual assault or sex industry, and older adults experiencing elder abuse Hours: Crisis line: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week All other assistance: Varies by service Email (888) 654-2288 Get Directions Visit Website More Details DOMESTIC VIOLENCE RESOURCE CENTER INC ADVOCACY & EMPOWERMENT PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides advocates to help support survivors of intimate partner domestic violence with protective order filing assistance, safety planning, resource referrals, basic needs support, court accompaniment, application assistance for the Address Confidentiality Program and more. Culturally-specific advocacy for LatinX and LGBTQ+ communities. All services are confidential. Eligibility: Qualifications for obtaining a restraining order in Washington County: Must be "related" to the perpetrator in one of the following ways: -Current or former spouse -Adult relative by blood, marriage, or adoption -Someone you live with or used to live with, in a sexually intimate relationship -Someone with whom you had a sexually intimate relationship within the past 2 years but never lived with The person you are filing against must have done one of the following things in the past 6 months: -Caused you bodily injury -Attempted to cause you bodily injury -Placed you in fear of imminent bodily injury -Caused you to engage in involuntary sexual relations by force or threat of force Qualifications for obtaining a stalking order in Washington County: There must be 2 incidents within the past 2 years that meet all of the following requirements: -Intentionally, knowingly, or recklessly engaging in repeated and unwanted contact with you or a member of your immediate family/household -Contact that causes you to feel coerced or alarmed -It is objectively reasonable for a person in your situation to have been alarmed or coerced by the contact -The repeated and unwanted contact causes you reasonable apprehension regarding your personal safety or the safety of members of your immediate family/household Hours: Office hours: Monday-Thursday 8:30am-4pm, Friday 8:30am-2pm Restraining order court is held Monday-Friday 1pm Stalking order court is held Monday/Wednesday/Friday 1pm Contested hearings: By appointment only Email (503) 640-5352 Get Directions Visit Website More Details DOMESTIC VIOLENCE SERVICES INC BOARDMAN ADVOCACY CENTER ADVOCACY SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides emergency shelter in Pendleton and Hermiston for survivors and their children, restraining order petition assistance, and help going to hospitals and court. 200 City Center Circle Boardman, OR 97818 Eligibility: Survivors of domestic, sexual, or dating violence and/or stalking Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm (541) 276-3322 Get Directions Visit Website More Details DOMESTIC VIOLENCE SERVICES INC HERMISTON ADVOCACY CENTER ADVOCACY SERVICES Print & restraining order petition assistance, and help going to hospitals and court. 1103 SE Court Place Pendleton, OR 97801 Eligibility: Survivors of domestic, sexual, or dating violence and/or stalking Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (541) 276-3322 Get Directions Visit Website More Details DOVE ADVOCACY CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides advocacy, protective order assistance, emergency shelter, safety planning, court and medical accompaniment, referrals, support groups, and a crisis line. 726 N 1st Street Lakeview, OR 97630 Eligibility: Survivors of domestic violence, sexual assault, stalking, dating violence, human trafficking, and elder abuse. Also serves individulas with disabilities who are victims of abuse. Hours: Office: Monday-Friday 8am-4pm Crisis line: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (541) 947-2449 Get Directions Visit Website More Details GATEWAY CENTER FOR DOMESTIC VIOLENCE SERVICES INTIMATE PARTNER VIOLENCE ADVOCACY AND SUPPORT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offer support by providing information and helping with forms related to safety concerns. Assist with crisis planning, restraining orders, crime reporting, mental health counseling, support groups, and cultural advocacy. Provides Safety and Stabilization Assessment (SSA) for domestic violence shelters or housing resources. SSA is required before accessing most direct domestic violence services. 10305 E Burnside Street Portland, OR 97216 Eligibility: Survivors of intimate partner violence and/or sexual assult Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-4pm Email (503) 988-6400 Get Directions Visit Website More Details HAVEN FROM DOMESTIC AND SEXUAL VIOLENCE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a crisis line, safety planning, short-term shelter, counseling, court advocacy, support groups, and referral to and advocacy with local agencies and other programs that offer long-term shelter. For survivors of domestic violence and sexual assault, provides assistance with temporary restraining and stalking orders. Does not provide intervention services for perpetrators of violence. 420 E 3rd Street The Dalles, OR 97058 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: -Crisis line: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week -Hours vary for other services Email (541) 296-1662 Get Directions Visit Website More Details HENDERSON HOUSE HENDERSON HOUSE ADVOCACY CENTER CRISIS INTERVENTION SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides domestic violence and sexual assault client advocacy services. Services include a crisis hotline, coordinated wraparound service, referrals, assistance with filling out protective orders, youth outreach support groups, weekly support groups for domestic violence and sexual violence survivors (including adult survivors of child sexual abuse), and an art therapy group. 610 SE 1st Street McMinnville, OR 97128 Eligibility: Survivors of interpersonal and/or sexual violence (including stalking), human trafficking, and their children who have experienced or witnessed abuse Hours: Crisis line: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Support groups: Tuesday/ Thursday 6pm-7:30pm All other services: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm Email (503) 472-1503 Get Directions Visit Website More Details HOPE AND SAFETY ALLIANCE CRISIS LINE AND SUPPORT CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a confidential crisis and support line for safety planning and referrals, a safe house, support groups, and community education to prevent intimate partner violence. Can assist with restraining orders and accompanying individuals to the Lane County Courthouse. Rural outreach provided by arrangement. 1577 Pearl Street Floor 2, Suite 200 Eugene, OR 97401 Eligibility: Survivors of intimate partner violence of any kind, regardless of gender, as well as their families and friends Hours: Crisis and Support Line: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Web Chat: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm Appointments: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm Email (541) 485-6513 Get Directions Visit Website More Details ILLINOIS VALLEY SAFE HOUSE ALLIANCE SURVIVOR ADVOCACY AND SUPPORT SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a range of advocacy and support to survivors of domestic violence, elder abuse, dating violence, trafficking, or sexual assault. Services include crisis intervention, court and medical accompaniment, peer advocacy, support groups, assistance with locating financial help, assistance with restraining and stalking orders, community education, referrals to meet emergency needs, and emergency shelter. 103 S Kerby Avenue Cave Junction, OR 97523 Eligibility: Survivors of domestic violence, elder abuse, dating violence, trafficking, or sexual assault Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm, closed federal holidays Email (541) 592-2515 Get Directions Visit Website More Details KLAMATH ADVOCACY CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a helpline, support groups, and a variety of supportive services for survivors of intimate partner violence, sexual assault, stalking, and trafficking. Provides emergency shelter services, based on availability, for survivors. Also provides in-person response to the emergency room for sexual assault survivors and assistance with protective and stalking orders. Every person who contacts Klamath Advocacy Center gets five essential services for safety and independence: safety plan, risk assessment, needs assessment, resource information, and next steps. PO Box 1358 Klamath Falls, OR 97601 Eligibility: Shelter: Women and women with minor children who are survivors of domestic/intimate partner violence or sexual assault All other services: Survivors of domestic/intimate partner violence, sexual assault, dating violence, and/or stalking Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (541) 884-0390 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OREGON LAW CENTER OREGON LAW CENTER LANE COUNTY LEGAL AID GENERAL LEGAL AID Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides general legal counseling and/or representation for low-income individuals who need assistance with routine legal matters (civil, non-criminal), usually regarding housing, public benefits, family, or elder law. Some locations may offer additional services. 101 E Broadway Suite 200 Eugene, OR 97401 Eligibility: Low-income individuals who are at or below 125% of the Federal Poverty Level (FPL) Hours: Office hours: Monday/Tuesday/Wednesday/Friday 9am-11:45am and 12:45pm-5pm Intake for new clients: Monday/Wednesday/Friday 1pm-3pm (541) 485-1017 100 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OREGON LAW CENTER OREGON LAW CENTER ONTARIO REGIONAL OFFICE GENERAL LEGAL AID Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides legal counseling and/or representation for low-income individuals who need assistance with routine legal matters (civil, non-criminal), usually regarding housing, public benefits, family, or elder law. Also provides legal assistance for survivors of domestic violence, including the obtainment of restraining orders. Some locations may offer additional services. 35 SE 5th Avenue Unit 1 Ontario, OR 97914 Eligibility: Low-income individuals who are at or below 125% of the Federal Poverty Level (FPL) Hours: Monday-Thursday 9am-12:30pm and 1:30pm-5pm Email (541) 889-3121 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SAVING GRACE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides comprehensive family violence and sexual assault services. Services include 24 hour crisis hotline, emergency shelter, individual crisis counseling for adults and children, weekly support groups for adults and children, restraining order assistance, hospital accompaniment, and supervised visitation and exchange. 990 NW Brooks Street Suite 1 Bend, OR 97703 Eligibility: Survivors of sexual assault or domestic violence, and friends and family of victims Hours: Crisis hotline: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week All other services by appointment only Email (541) 389-7021 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SAVING GRACE SAVING GRACE MADRAS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides comprehensive family violence and sexual assault services. Services include 24 hour crisis hotline, emergency shelter, individual crisis counseling for adults and children, weekly support groups for adults and children, restraining order assistance, hospital accompaniment, and supervised visitation and exchange. Eligibility: Survivors of sexual assault or domestic violence, and friends and family of victims Hours: Crisis hotline: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week All other services by appointment only Email (541) 475-1880 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SAVING GRACE SAVING GRACE PRINEVILLE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides comprehensive family violence and sexual assault services. Services include 24 hour crisis hotline, emergency shelter, individual crisis counseling for adults and children, weekly support groups for adults and children, restraining order assistance, hospital accompaniment, and supervised visitation and exchange. Eligibility: Survivors of sexual assault or domestic violence, and friends and family of victims Hours: Crisis hotline: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week All other services by appointment only Email (541) 416-2114 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SAVING GRACE SAVING GRACE REDMOND Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides comprehensive family violence and sexual assault services. Services include 24 hour crisis hotline, emergency shelter, individual crisis counseling for adults and children, weekly support groups for adults and children, restraining order assistance, hospital accompaniment, and supervised visitation and exchange. Eligibility: Survivors of sexual assault or domestic violence, and friends and family of victims Hours: Crisis hotline: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week All other services by appointment only Email (541) 504-2550 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SEXUAL ASSAULT RESOURCE CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides in-person and telephone accompaniment to hospitals in Washington County, law enforcement agencies, schools, clinics, shelters, and other secure locations, supporting individuals during medical exams, law enforcement interviews, and other related situations. Limited case management services including ongoing emotional support, safety planning, assistance through criminal and civil justice processes, address confidentiality, crime victim compensation, housing advocacy, protection orders, and referrals to additional resources. Also offers limited peer-led support and educational groups, as well as prevention education and community presentations. 100 NE Campton Drive Hillsboro, OR 97006 Eligibility: Individuals who live, work, attend school, or had sexual assault incident occur in Washington County Hours: Phone: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week All other services: No standard hours Email (503) 640-5311 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SIUSLAW OUTREACH SERVICES SIUSLAW AREA RESOURCE CENTER DOMESTIC VIOLENCE SUPPORT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a resource, referral, and crisis line that primarily focuses on survivors of domestic/sexual violence, and human trafficking and stalking. In addition to providing a crisis line, offers support in the form of a domestic violence safe house, protection orders, advocacy, and support groups. 1536 W 12th Street Florence, OR 97439 Eligibility: Primarily focuses on survivors of domestic/sexual violence; other emergency calls are accepted Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (541) 997-4444 24- Get Directions Visit Website More Details THE HARBOR PROTECTIVE SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Helps survivors of domestic violence, sexual assault, and/or stalking obtain legal protection. 801 Commercial Street Astoria, OR 97103 Eligibility: Age 14 or older Hours: Advocacy Office: Monday/Tuesday/Thursday/Friday 9am-4pm, Wednesday 9am-noon, closed 1st Wednesday of every month. Online Chat and Text: Monday-Friday 10am-5pm Email (503) 325-5735 Get Directions Visit Website More Details VOLUNTEERS OF AMERICA OREGON HOME FREE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a domestic violence and sexual intervention program that includes services such as: -Emergency Services: Hotline, information and referral, and telephone advocacy -Housing Services: Home visits, long-term advocacy and support, help to locate and maintain safe housing -Domestic and sexual violence healthcare and advocacy -Court and legal advocacy with a restraining order hotline -Economic Empowerment: Credit building and repair advocacy, savings, interview skills, employment seekers group, barrier reduction assistance, and Rent Well landlord-tenant education Motel vouchers are only available for those in emergencies who are actively fleeing domestic violence. Availability is dependent upon funding. Eligibility: Survivors of domestic and sexual violence Hours: Emergency Hotline: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm Restraining Order Services: Monday-Friday 8am-2pm Office: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (503) 235-8655 Get Directions Visit Website More Details YOUTH, RIGHTS & JUSTICE FAMILY DEFENSE PROJECT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Works with parents whose children are at risk of entering the foster care system. Addresses legal and resource needs such as child custody, legal counseling, and restraining orders. Focus is to empower families, protect children, and prevent foster care placement. 1785 NE Sandy Boulevard Suite 300 Portland, OR 97232 Eligibility: Households that have an open ODHS case with risk of child removal Hours: Monday-Friday 8:30am-5pm Email (503) 803-8640 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CENTER AGAINST RAPE AND DOMESTIC VIOLENCE DOMESTIC VIOLENCE & SEXUAL ASSAULT SERVICES CLACKAMAS WOMEN'S SERVICES DOMESTIC VIOLENCE AND SEXUAL ASSAULT SERVICES DOMESTIC VIOLENCE SERVICES INC ADVOCACY SERVICES DOMESTIC VIOLENCE SERVICES INC ADVOCACY SERVICES DOMESTIC VIOLENCE SERVICES INC ADVOCACY SERVICES DOMESTIC VIOLENCE SERVICES INC ADVOCACY SERVICES DOMESTIC VIOLENCE SERVICES INC ADVOCACY SERVICES DOVE ADVOCACY CENTER DOVE ADVOCACY CENTER GATEWAY CENTER FOR DOMESTIC VIOLENCE SERVICES INTIMATE PARTNER VIOLENCE ADVOCACY AND SUPPORT HAVEN FROM DOMESTIC AND SEXUAL VIOLENCE HAVEN FROM DOMESTIC AND SEXUAL VIOLENCE HENDERSON HOUSE CRISIS INTERVENTION SERVICES HOPE AND SAFETY ALLIANCE CRISIS LINE AND SUPPORT CENTER ILLINOIS VALLEY SAFE HOUSE ALLIANCE SURVIVOR ADVOCACY AND SUPPORT SERVICES KLAMATH ADVOCACY CENTER KLAMATH ADVOCACY CENTER OREGON LAW CENTER GENERAL LEGAL AID OREGON LAW CENTER GENERAL LEGAL AID SAVING GRACE SAVING GRACE SEXUAL ASSAULT RESOURCE CENTER SEXUAL ASSAULT RESOURCE CENTER SIUSLAW OUTREACH SERVICES DOMESTIC VIOLENCE SUPPORT THE HARBOR PROTECTIVE SERVICES YOUTH, RIGHTS & JUSTICE FAMILY DEFENSE PROJECT
